jokes.text: I don't belong to a health club. I have a limit on naked men I see in one day.
jokes.setup: Jerry explaining why he doesn't belong to a health club
jokes.punchline: The unexpected reason - a limit on naked men
jokes.explanation: Strong opening joke with unexpected premise and perfect setup/punchline structure

jokes.text: Zero. That's it.
jokes.setup: The limit on naked men he sees
jokes.punchline: The limit is zero - complete avoidance
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy - two words that escalate the absurdity

jokes.text: What is this, the Kremlin?
jokes.setup: Complaining about high security at health clubs
jokes.punchline: Comparing gym security to Soviet-era fortress
jokes.explanation: Good observational premise with absurd comparison

jokes.text: Is this a big problem, people stealing exercise?
jokes.setup: Questioning gym security measures
jokes.punchline: The absurd concept of 'stealing exercise'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist premise - 'stealing exercise' is a perfect Seinfeld concept

jokes.text: And what if they catch the person? What then? They run. It's aerobic. Makes it even worse.
jokes.setup: What happens when they catch exercise thieves
jokes.punchline: Running away is exercise, making the theft worse
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation of the exercise theft premise with perfect logic

jokes.text: Unless someone's stretching in front of me in a leotard, then I can go an hour.
jokes.setup: Jerry lasting 10 minutes on StairMaster
jokes.punchline: Motivation comes from ogling women in leotards
jokes.explanation: Predictable male gaze humor, functional but not original

jokes.text: That's why it's called the StairMaster. You get up there and you stare.
jokes.setup: Explaining gym equipment motivation
jokes.punchline: StairMaster = Stare Master wordplay
jokes.explanation: Solid wordplay but somewhat obvious pun

jokes.text: You know, they're fake.
jokes.setup: Jerry praising Sidra as tremendous
jokes.punchline: Elaine's blunt assessment of her breasts
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection and character-specific bluntness from Elaine

jokes.text: You know how you always brag how you can spot a lesbian? I'm not bragging. I happen to have a very keen lesbian eye.
jokes.setup: Elaine accusing Jerry of bragging about lesbian-spotting
jokes.punchline: Jerry's matter-of-fact defense of his 'keen lesbian eye'
jokes.explanation: Great character moment - Jerry's odd pride in bizarre skill

jokes.text: Leg man? Why would I be a leg man? I don't need legs. I have legs.
jokes.setup: Elaine calling Jerry a leg man
jokes.punchline: Jerry's logic that you don't desire what you already have
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Jerry logic - absurd but internally consistent reasoning

jokes.text: Yes. Go in there. Do a little investigative journalism.
jokes.setup: Elaine asking if she should spy on Sidra in the sauna
jokes.punchline: Jerry frames creepy spying as noble journalism
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Jerry's selfish manipulation

jokes.text: Can't go left.
jokes.setup: George's dating problems due to hand preference
jokes.punchline: Simple statement of George's physical limitation
jokes.explanation: Establishes absurd running gag about handedness in romance

jokes.text: I'm lefty. Can't go right.
jokes.setup: George can't go left
jokes.punchline: Jerry has the opposite problem
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the handedness premise

jokes.text: No, we just play defense.
jokes.setup: Question about women going left or right romantically
jokes.punchline: Women don't initiate, they just defend
jokes.explanation: Sharp sports metaphor for dating dynamics

jokes.text: I'll shout.
jokes.setup: Betsy saying she doesn't hear well, prefers her seat
jokes.punchline: George's simple solution to her hearing problem
jokes.explanation: Perfect George moment - selfish but delivered with confidence

jokes.text: In the whole world right now there's maybe three emergencies. Why would you think on this planet that you're one of those three?
jokes.setup: Betsy worrying about missing phone call being emergency
jokes.punchline: George's mathematical approach to global emergencies
jokes.explanation: Excellent George logic - absurd but delivered with total conviction

jokes.text: All right, maybe four.
jokes.setup: His confident assertion about three emergencies
jokes.punchline: Immediately proven wrong, adjusts his count
jokes.explanation: Perfect immediate callback with deadpan adjustment

jokes.text: Good sweat. Beads of sweat. Sweating bullets.
jokes.setup: Elaine commenting on her sweating in sauna
jokes.punchline: Increasingly dramatic descriptions of perspiration
jokes.explanation: Functional escalation but lacks originality

jokes.text: She's got a rack on her chest.
jokes.setup: Commenting on why Sidra bothers with towel
jokes.punchline: Crude description of prominent breasts
jokes.explanation: Crude humor without much craft or character insight

jokes.text: It's like finding out Mickey Mantle corked his bat.
jokes.setup: Jerry's disappointment about fake breasts
jokes.punchline: Comparing to baseball cheating scandal
jokes.explanation: Great sports metaphor connecting authenticity in romance and sports

jokes.text: You don't unhook anything to get to a nose...and no man has ever tried to look up a woman's nostrils.
jokes.setup: Comparing nose jobs to breast implants
jokes.punchline: Absurd imagery of nostril exploration
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd escalation of the nose/breast comparison

jokes.text: Sometimes when I think you're the shallowest man I've ever met...you somehow manage to drain a little more out of the pool.
jokes.setup: Jerry's shallow comments about authenticity
jokes.punchline: Extended metaphor about Jerry's bottomless shallowness
jokes.explanation: Exceptional insult with perfect extended metaphor - could be series-best line

jokes.text: You got five million Moslems after you. You wanna stay in pretty good shape.
jokes.setup: Kramer claiming to see Salman Rushdie at gym
jokes.punchline: Logical reason why Rushdie would work out
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about real-world death threats, well-executed

jokes.text: I put them on a dental chair.
jokes.setup: Jerry saying George puts women on pedestals
jokes.punchline: George's more clinical/professional perspective
jokes.explanation: Perfect George line - reveals his dentistry and clinical nature

jokes.text: You're a funny guy. Never heard that before.
jokes.setup: Someone calling George funny
jokes.punchline: George's sarcastic response about his reputation
jokes.explanation: Standard sarcasm, functional but not particularly clever

jokes.text: Her aunt dying is the best thing for you. It's like 10 dates in one shot.
jokes.setup: Jerry advising George about going to funeral
jokes.punchline: Quantifying romantic benefit of family death
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor - Jerry's callous calculation is perfectly in character

jokes.text: You're getting sandwiches. You're the rock.
jokes.setup: Describing George's role at funeral
jokes.punchline: Building to climactic metaphor of George as 'the rock'
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with Jerry's manipulative coaching

jokes.text: Oh, look who's here. Silicon Valley.
jokes.setup: Seeing Sidra in the sauna
jokes.punchline: Nickname playing on breast implants and tech region
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay connecting geography and anatomy

jokes.text: He would have made a great Nazi.
jokes.setup: Describing Jerry's obsession with neatness and order
jokes.punchline: Extreme historical comparison
jokes.explanation: Dark comparison that's somewhat predictable for the era

jokes.text: I touched them.
jokes.setup: Explaining how she fell into Sidra
jokes.punchline: Blunt admission of accidental groping
jokes.explanation: Peak cringe moment delivered with perfect economy

jokes.text: If it hadn't been for them, I could have really injured myself.
jokes.setup: Explaining the breast-touching incident
jokes.punchline: Framing breasts as safety equipment
jokes.explanation: Absurd rationalization that's perfectly Elaine

jokes.text: I've touched mine.
jokes.setup: Jerry saying she has no breast-touching experience
jokes.punchline: Elaine's matter-of-fact response about self-knowledge
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery of obvious point

jokes.text: All right. Anyway, I think they're real, and if they are, they are spectacular.
jokes.setup: Elaine's final assessment of Sidra's breasts
jokes.punchline: The word 'spectacular' as ultimate endorsement
jokes.explanation: Sets up the episode's most famous callback line

jokes.text: What kind of a sick person would do a thing like that?
jokes.setup: Airline agent explaining people abuse bereavement fares
jokes.punchline: Jerry's faux outrage while literally doing exactly that
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic hypocrisy - Jerry's moral outrage while committing the crime

jokes.text: If you were drowning and I threw you a life preserver...you think you could tell me if it was an inflatable?
jokes.setup: Defending Elaine's assessment while grabbing for support
jokes.punchline: Analogy about tactile knowledge under duress
jokes.explanation: Excellent extended metaphor connecting emergency situations and tactile knowledge

jokes.text: If that's Rushdie, they're real. If they're real, that's Rushdie.
jokes.setup: Confusion over Salman Rushdie and Sidra's breasts
jokes.punchline: Illogical connection between two unrelated mysteries
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd logic connecting two plot threads

jokes.text: Well, I'm the boyfriend. Otherwise, what's the point of being the boyfriend. This is where you have to be when you're the boyfriend.
jokes.setup: Family thanking George for coming to funeral
jokes.punchline: George's repetitive, self-important explanation of boyfriend duties
jokes.explanation: Perfect George moment - self-important and oblivious to appropriate tone

jokes.text: Comfort schmomfort.
jokes.setup: Father praising George for comforting Betsy
jokes.punchline: George's dismissive Yiddish-inflected response
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing George's inability to accept praise gracefully

jokes.text: Bass, Jerry. Instead of salmon, he went with bass. He just substituted one fish for another.
jokes.setup: Jerry not believing the man is Salman Rushdie
jokes.punchline: Kramer's fish-based conspiracy theory
jokes.explanation: Excellent Kramer logic - absurd but delivered with total conviction

jokes.text: Look, you idiot, first of all, it's 'Salman,' not 'salmon.'
jokes.setup: Kramer's fish theory
jokes.punchline: Jerry's exasperated correction of basic pronunciation
jokes.explanation: Functional straight-man response but not particularly clever

jokes.text: Did you just double dip that chip?
jokes.setup: George eating at funeral
jokes.punchline: Accusation of double-dipping violation
jokes.explanation: Introduces the famous double-dip concept that became cultural phenomenon

jokes.text: It's like putting your whole mouth right in the dip.
jokes.setup: Explaining the double-dip violation
jokes.punchline: Visceral description of the contamination
jokes.explanation: Perfect explanation that makes the violation viscerally disgusting

jokes.text: Well, I'm sorry, Timmy...but I don't dip that way.
jokes.setup: Being told not to double-dip
jokes.punchline: George's stubborn assertion of dipping independence
jokes.explanation: Perfect George moment - defiant about something completely wrong

jokes.text: I like sitting to the left of people. Makes me feel like I'm driving.
jokes.setup: Jerry asking to switch seats on plane
jokes.punchline: Bizarre psychological explanation for seating preference
jokes.explanation: Good Jerry observation about psychological quirks

jokes.text: Huge. Just coming out the top of her dress. They were choking her. I hear that's how she died.
jokes.setup: Discussing Jayne Mansfield's breasts
jokes.punchline: Claiming they caused her death by strangulation
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about celebrity death, moderately effective

jokes.text: Well, I think you'll find out soon enough.
jokes.setup: Jerry asking what to tell people about her breasts
jokes.punchline: Sidra's suggestive response about intimate discovery
jokes.explanation: Standard sexual innuendo without much craft

jokes.text: I don't want your boys down there.
jokes.setup: Kramer wanting to borrow bathing suit
jokes.punchline: Jerry's euphemistic reference to genitals
jokes.explanation: Decent euphemism for male anatomy, moderately clever

jokes.text: Your boys should stay in their neighborhood.
jokes.setup: Continuing the 'boys' euphemism
jokes.punchline: Extended metaphor about territorial boundaries
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the euphemism with territorial metaphor

jokes.text: I think you're both mentally ill.
jokes.setup: Learning about Jerry and Elaine's breast investigation
jokes.punchline: Direct assessment of their mental state
jokes.explanation: Functional callback but not particularly clever

jokes.text: And by the way, they're real and they're spectacular.
jokes.setup: Sidra's final statement before leaving
jokes.punchline: Echoing Elaine's earlier assessment with vindication
jokes.explanation: Iconic callback line that became cultural touchstone - perfect structure and delivery

jokes.text: A picture of me next to the coffin.
jokes.setup: George trying to prove death in family for airline discount
jokes.punchline: His pathetic attempt at documentation
jokes.explanation: Perfect George moment - completely missing the point of documentation

jokes.text: Give me the cheesecake, crank me up to nine...and put a scoop of ice cream on the side.
jokes.setup: Imagining restaurant liposuction service
jokes.punchline: Ordering procedure like a menu item with sides
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist premise combining indulgence with instant correction
